finding: Exact-byte digests and semantic JSON identity solve different problems. RFC 8785 canonicalization provides an invariant UTF-8 representation for I-JSON values, allowing equivalent objects with different property insertion order to share a semantic digest while preserving array order and primitive values.
design_implication: Evidence systems should retain both the original-byte digest and a separately labeled canonical semantic digest. Neither digest proves the truth of the evidence claim; each only binds a different representation boundary.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files
cases: equivalent object property order produces identical digest
non-finite numbers, bigint, lone surrogates, custom objects, and cycles fail closed

evidence_strength: committed deterministic implementation with primary-specification test vector and adversarial tests
uncertainty: The implementation accepts already-parsed JavaScript values and therefore cannot detect duplicate object property names that a parser may already have collapsed.
Cross-language conformance has not been tested.
RFC 8785 is informational rather than Standards Track.
falsification_route: Run the committed implementation against the RFC 8785 Appendix B number vectors and an independent conforming JCS implementation. Any canonical byte mismatch falsifies conformance for that input.
trust_limit: Canonical equality is not evidence that two claims are true, authorized, fresh, or produced by a trusted builder.
